reason: Files NOT on main branch (git show main:.playwright-cli/ fails). All 7 session-artifact files are committed only in this PR. Certain real issue.
schedule.db-shm and schedule.db-wal NOT on main, committed by this PR. SQLite WAL files are runtime artifacts. .gitignore pattern 'schedule.db' does not match these names. Certain real issue.
frontend/dist/index.html tracked in this branch, NOT on main. Build output committed. Certain real issue.
PR diff confirms both aliyun http:// and tsinghua https:// mirrors were added. HTTP mirror is a supply-chain MITM risk. Pre-existing=false. Real security concern, not a nitpick.

JobPipeline.tsx is a new PR file. renderDetail() passes src="", script="等待生成...", checks={null} to MediaPlayer/ScriptPreview/SubtitleEditor. No real job data flows through. Real functional gap, not scaffolding — component exists to render data but gets none.

Confirmed by reading code. Line 25 sets activeStepKey=j.phase (e.g. 'script_generating') but PIPELINE_STEPS keys are 'script_gen'. Line 31 comparison step.key===activeStepKey never matches on initial load. Clear functional bug, certain.
PipelineSidebar lines 63-73 render three buttons (pause, retry, logs) with zero onClick handlers. Pure dead UI. Confirmed by reading the file.

api_jobs.py is a new PR file. reject_review() (line 137) only updates review_status='rejected', never changes phase. Job stays in script_review/asset_review/final_review forever. Highly certain this is a bug — no reconciliation logic rescues it.

retry_job() calls enqueue_demo_job() but never save_job() to update phase. Verified enqueue_demo_job only adds to in-memory queue, does not persist state. Asymmetric with pause_job which does save_job(). Highly likely real bug — stored phase becomes inconsistent with queued state.

ConfigPage.tsx is a new PR file. Both load() and handleSectionSave() wrap API calls in try/catch with empty catch blocks. Silent failure: user sees no error on save failure. Real UX regression. Not a linter issue, not pedantic.

JobTable.tsx line 32 renders j.product. Confirmed list_jobs() in repository.py (line 55-71) only returns job_id/phase/review_status — no product field. j.product will be undefined. Highly certain this is a real data-flow gap.

api_projects.py line 69 calls repo.create_project(project_id) without name=, defaulting to empty string. Edge case: only triggers if upload_asset is called for non-existent project. Real bug in that scenario, but unlikely in normal flow.

validate_provider_payload is literally a one-line passthrough to _merge_payload. Misleading name (no validation, just merge). Not a functional bug. Somewhat pedantic naming critique. Linter would not catch. Low confidence it matters.

validate_provider_payload imported in config.py line 13 but never called in that file. A linter (ruff, pyright) would catch this. Per rubric, issues a linter would catch should not be scored. Score 0.
